'''Andando''' was the innermost [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Ando system/Legends|Ando system]], a [[Star system/Legends|star system]]<ref name="Planet of the Walrus Men" /> located within the [[Lambda sector/Legends|Lambda sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="Planet of the Walrus Men" /> and [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] region.<ref name="The Slice" /> [[Ando/Legends|Ando's]] sister planet,<ref name="Aqualish">{{DB|species|aqualish|Aqualish}}</ref> it [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Ando (star)|Ando]] and had no [[Moon/Legends|moons]].<ref name="Planet of the Walrus Men" /> Andando was ravaged<ref name="Aqualish" /> in the aftermath of an [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Aqualish Civil War|war]]<ref name="Ando">{{CSWECite|I|30|Ando}}</ref><ref name="CSWE Aqualish">{{CSWECite|I|38|Aqualish}}</ref> several [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] after the [[Species/Legends|species]] discovered [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel.<ref name="Second Edition">[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races'', Second Edition]]</ref> They systematically stripped the planet of its resources<ref name="Dangerous Covenants">''[[Dangerous Covenants]]''</ref> and blasted its surface<ref name="Ando" /> into molten rock,<ref name="Planet of the Walrus Men" /> rendering Andando uninhabitable.<ref name="Dangerous Covenants" />

==Behind the scenes==
Andando was first mentioned, albeit indirectly, in ''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]'', a [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] supplement written by [[Troy Denning]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and released in [[1989]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races">''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]''</ref> It was first identified in "[[Ando: Planet of the Walrus Men]]," a "[[Planet Hoppers]]" article written by [[Cory J. Herndon]] for the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' and published on [[July 3]], [[2003]].<ref name="Planet of the Walrus Men" /> ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', a [[2009]] reference book written by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]], placed the Ando system, and therefore Andando, in grid square Q-15.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
